115.code: for(p = G[i].link; p != NULL; pri = p, p = p->next)
115.reason: [In the first iteration, 'pri' is used uninitialized when p != G[i].link. Also, 'free(p)' inside the loop causes use-after-free because 'p' is advanced in the for-loop header after being freed.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [In the first iteration, 'pri' is used uninitialized when p != G[i].link. Also, 'free(p)' inside the loop causes use-after-free because 'p' is advanced in the for-loop header after being freed.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [If G[i].link is NULL, this loop won't run, but 'i' is never incremented in that case, causing infinite loop or crash]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [If G[i].link is NULL, the loop body won't execute, but 'i' is never incremented in that case, causing infinite loop or skipped vertices]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [In the first iteration, 'pri' is used uninitialized when p != G[i].link. Also, 'free(p)' inside the loop causes use-after-free because 'p' is advanced in the for-loop header after being freed.]

121.reason: [The pointer 'p' is freed inside the loop, but then used in the loop increment 'p = p->next', causing use-after-free. Also, 'pri' may be used uninitialized if the loop body runs at least once.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Freeing 'p' here makes the next iteration of the for-loop use a dangling pointer since 'p' is set to p->next in the loop update clause after this free.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Freeing 'p' inside the loop invalidates the pointer used in loop increment 'p = p->next', causing use-after-free]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Freeing 'p' inside the loop invalidates the pointer used in 'p = p->next', causing use-after-free and undefined behavior]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The pointer 'p' is freed inside the loop, but then used in the loop increment 'p = p->next', causing use-after-free. Also, 'pri' may be used uninitialized if the loop body runs at least once.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Freeing 'p' here leads to accessing freed memory in the next iteration of the for-loop since 'p = p->next' uses the already freed pointer.]

122.reason: [Incrementing 'i' inside the inner loop causes skipping of vertices and potential infinite loop or out-of-bounds access. 'i' should only be incremented in the outer loop.]
